description:
Smart Label Sync — Auto-Create & Populate Matching Outlook Folders

Extend the Smart Labels feature to create and maintain a corresponding Outlook folder in the user's Exchange mailbox for every smart label. As incoming messages are evaluated against smart label rules, matching messages are automatically moved into the folder that mirrors the label, keeping Outlook and Krisp in sync.

Requirements:

Folder Provisioning on Label Creation
- When a user creates a smart label, call the Microsoft Graph API to create a mail folder under the user's Exchange mailbox with the same name as the smart label
- Store the Graph API mailFolderId returned from the creation response alongside the smart label record in the database so subsequent operations can reference it without re-querying
- Handle naming conflicts gracefully: if a folder with that name already exists in Outlook, retrieve and link to the existing folder rather than failing or creating a duplicate
- Propagate label renames to the corresponding Outlook folder name via a Graph API PATCH call

Message Matching & Movement
- After a smart label's search/filter rules are evaluated against an incoming or indexed message, call the Microsoft Graph API (POST /messages/{id}/move) to move any matching message into the label's linked Outlook folder
- Ensure the move operation is idempotent: if a message has already been moved to the correct folder, skip the operation without error
- Handle multi-label matches with a defined tie-breaking or priority strategy (e.g., first matching label wins, or highest-priority label wins) so a message is not moved to multiple folders simultaneously
- Queue failed move operations for retry with exponential back-off; surface persistent failures in the structured error log

Folder Lifecycle Management
- When a smart label is deleted leave the folder intact but unlinked from Krisp
- When a smart label is archived or disabled, stop routing new messages into its folder but do not remove the folder or its existing contents
- When a smart label is renamed, rename the corresponding Outlook folder to match

Data Model
- Add a graph_folder_id column to the smart labels table to persist the Exchange mailFolderId
- Add a synced_to_outlook_folder boolean or enum status to track provisioning state (pending, synced, failed, unlinked)

Graph API & Auth Constraints
- All folder and message operations must use the authenticated user's Microsoft Graph token obtained via the existing Graph API integration; no service-account impersonation
- Respect Microsoft Graph API rate limits; batch folder-move calls where the API permits
- Scope requirements: ensure Mail.ReadWrite is present in the OAuth consent scope in addition to any existing scopes

Edge Cases to Consider
- User revokes Microsoft 365 OAuth access after folders are already created — detect auth failure, pause sync, and surface a reconnect prompt
- Smart label rule is edited — re-evaluate previously received messages against the updated rule and move newly matching messages to the folder; do not un-move messages that no longer match (treat prior moves as permanent to avoid disrupting the user's Outlook state)
- Outlook folder is deleted externally by the user — detect the missing folder on next sync, re-provision it, and update the stored graph_folder_id
- Message is already in the correct folder (e.g., moved manually by the user) — skip without error
- Exchange mailbox storage quota is near limit — catch Graph API 507 responses and surface a warning rather than silently failing
title: Implement Smart Label Sync with Outlook Folder Auto-Creation

summary: When a user sets up a smart rule on a workspace page, they may have multiple email accounts and need to choose which one to use for Outlook folder sync. The page also needs to indicate whether it's linked to an Outlook folder.
